testcase.name: Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
When the operator test "equals" is run
Then the output should equal "true"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "not-my-test-value"
When the operator test "equals" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "98"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "98"
When the operator test "equals" is run
Then the output should equal "true"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "98"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "10"
When the operator test "equals" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "-test-v"
When the operator test "contains" is run
Then the output should equal "true"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "nope"
When the operator test "contains" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
When the operator test "contains" is run
Then the output should equal "true"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value-two"
When the operator test "contains" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "my-test"
When the operator test "startsWith" is run
Then the output should equal "true"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "-test-v"
When the operator test "startsWith" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
When the operator test "startsWith" is run
Then the output should equal "true"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value-two"
When the operator test "startsWith" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "test-value"
When the operator test "endsWith" is run
Then the output should equal "true"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "-test-v"
When the operator test "endsWith" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
When the operator test "endsWith" is run
Then the output should equal "true"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "long-my-test-value"
When the operator test "endsWith" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "my.test.+"
When the operator test "matches" is run
Then the output should equal "true"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "wont-match"
When the operator test "matches" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "^[A-z\-]+$"
When the operator test "matches" is run
Then the output should equal "true"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "^[A-z]+$"
When the operator test "matches" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "my-test-value"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "doesn't matter"
When the operator test "fakeOperator" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "two"
And the attribute is an array of values "one, two, three"
When the operator test "in" is run
Then the output should equal "true"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "four"
And the attribute is an array of values "one, two, three"
When the operator test "in" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "four"
And the attribute is an array of values "one, two, three"
When the operator test "notIn" is run
Then the output should equal "true"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "two"
And the attribute is an array of values "one, two, three"
When the operator test "notIn" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "number" with the value of "9000"
And the attribute is a "number" with the value of "9000"
When the operator test "equals" is run
Then the output should equal "true"

And the attribute is a "number" with the value of "8999"
When the operator test "equals" is run
Then the output should equal "false"

And the attribute is a "number" with the value of "9000.1"
When the operator test "equals" is run
Then the output should equal "true"
Given the target is a "number" with the value of "9000.2"
And the attribute is a "number" with the value of "9000.1"
When the operator test "equals" is run
Then the output should equal "false"

And the attribute is a "number" with the value of "8999"
When the operator test "greaterThan" is run
Then the output should equal "true"

And the attribute is a "number" with the value of "9001"
When the operator test "greaterThan" is run
Then the output should equal "false"

When the operator test "greaterThan" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "number" with the value of "9000.2"

When the operator test "greaterThan" is run
Then the output should equal "true"

And the attribute is a "number" with the value of "9001"
When the operator test "greaterThan" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "number" with the value of "9000.5"
And the attribute is a "number" with the value of "9000.5"
When the operator test "greaterThan" is run
Then the output should equal "false"

And the attribute is a "number" with the value of "8999"
When the operator test "greaterThanOrEqual" is run
Then the output should equal "true"

Then the output should equal "false"

Then the output should equal "true"

Then the output should equal "false"

When the operator test "greaterThanOrEqual" is run
Then the output should equal "true"

Then the output should equal "false"

Then the output should equal "false"

Then the output should equal "false"

Given the target is a "string" with the value of "2017-03-09T02:39:46.182Z"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "2017-04-09T02:39:46.182Z"
When the operator test "before" is run
Then the output should equal "true"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "2017-03-09T02:39:46.182Z"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "2017-02-09T02:39:46.182Z"
When the operator test "before" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "2017-03-09T02:39:46.182Z"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "2017-03-09T02:39:46.182Z"
When the operator test "before" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "2017-03-09T02:39:46.182Z"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "2017-04-09T02:39:46.182Z"
When the operator test "after" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "2017-03-09T02:39:46.182Z"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "2017-02-09T02:39:46.182Z"
When the operator test "after" is run
Then the output should equal "true"
Given the target is a "string" with the value of "2017-03-09T02:39:46.182Z"
And the attribute is a "string" with the value of "2017-03-09T02:39:46.182Z"
When the operator test "after" is run
Then the output should equal "false"
Given the feature "my-feature" with an offVariantKey "off", a default key of "on" is disabled
When the feature is evaluated with a user "user"
Then the evaluated variant should be "off"
Given the feature "my-feature" with an offVariantKey "off", a default key of "on" is enabled
When the feature is evaluated with a user "user"
Then the evaluated variant should be "on"
Given the salt is "1", the feature is "f1" and the id is "alice"
When the variant value is calculated
Then the hash value calculated should equal "de5ce0fbc583fd8"
And the result from the variant calculation should be 9
Given the salt is "1", the feature is "f1" and the id is "bob"
When the variant value is calculated
Then the hash value calculated should equal "8ecddc9f392dc35"
And the result from the variant calculation should be 14
Given the salt is "2", the feature is "f1" and the id is "alice"
When the variant value is calculated
Then the hash value calculated should equal "e31eff9e88214f2"
And the result from the variant calculation should be 71
Given the salt is "2", the feature is "f1" and the id is "bob"
When the variant value is calculated
Then the hash value calculated should equal "591e96e46fc1dad"
And the result from the variant calculation should be 58
Given the salt is "3", the feature is "f1" and the id is "alice"
When the variant value is calculated
Then the hash value calculated should equal "05ad8a286f0b0bb"
And the result from the variant calculation should be 36
Given the salt is "3", the feature is "f1" and the id is "bob"
When the variant value is calculated
Then the hash value calculated should equal "9bc2af62801255d"
And the result from the variant calculation should be 2

When the rule is matched against the user
Then the result from the match should be true

Then the result from the match should be true

Given the variant value of 50
And the variant splits are
When the variant split key is calculated
Then the resulting variant should be "off"
Given the variant value of 50
And the variant splits are
When the variant split key is calculated
Then the resulting variant should be "on"
Given the variant value of 11
And the variant splits are
When the variant split key is calculated
Then the resulting variant should be "on"
Given the variant value of 9
And the variant splits are
When the variant split key is calculated
Then the resulting variant should be "off"
Given the variant value of 1
And the variant splits are
When the variant split key is calculated
Then the resulting variant should be "on"
Given the variant value of 30
And the variant splits are
When the variant split key is calculated
Then the resulting variant should be "on"
Given the variant value of 50
And the variant splits are
When the variant split key is calculated
Then the resulting variant should be "alpha"
Given the variant value of 61
And the variant splits are
When the variant split key is calculated
Then the resulting variant should be "beta"
Given the variant value of 100
And the variant splits are
When the variant split key is calculated
Then the resulting variant should be "betav2"
Given there is access to the User Builder module
When the builder is initialised with the id "user"
And the user is built using the builder
Then the result user should have an id "user"
And the result user should have no attributes
Given there is access to the User Builder module
When the builder is initialised with the id "user"
And the builder is given the following attributes
And the user is built using the builder
Then the result user should have an id "user"
And the result user should have a attribute with key "age" and value "21"
And the result user should have a attribute with key "type" and value "beta"
And the result user should have a attribute with key "featureflow.user.id" and value "user"
And the result user should have a attribute with key "featureflow.date" and current datetime in iso8601
Given there is access to the User Builder module
When the builder is initialised with the id ""
Then the builder should throw an error
